behavior_summary: This script implements a movable/interactive platform item. The main 'U8P_PLATFORM::use' process gates platform movement by checking the item's quality/state and the total surface weight on the platform, allowing a movement routine when permitted and playing a denial sound otherwise.

U8P_PLATFORM::func0131 is the core movement/rotation routine: it sets the platform state, plays a confirmation SFX, computes Z offsets (using the platform's Z and a direction/step derived from an input/param), performs bounds checks, and iterates over an ethereal/top item stack to push/pop and cycle item types while repositioning items vertically. If a weight/limit or bounds check fails, it sets a fallback quality, may invert/abort movement, and plays a denial SFX.

U8P_PLATFORM::gotHit responds to external strikes: when a nonzero source object is provided and that object is on the platform and heavy enough, it delegates to a hit-response routine (PLATFORM::func0080) and suspends further processing.
notable_control_flow: U8P_PLATFORM::use: read Item::getQuality(this) into local1; if quality == 0x02 do nothing; else if quality == 0x01 call U8P_PLATFORM::func0131; else check Item::getSurfaceWeight(this) <= 0x64 to decide whether to call U8P_PLATFORM::func0131 or play a denial SFX (playSFX category 0x64, index 0x29).

U8P_PLATFORM::func0131: sets Item::setQuality(this, 0x02) then uses a param-derived signed step to compute target Z = Item::getZ(this) + step; aborts if target Z outside bounds (below 0x10 or above 0x38), in which case it sets a different quality and plays a denial sound; otherwise iterates items from getEtherealTop, computes per-item target Z (adjusting by ±0x08 alignment), cycles item types (pattern involving 0x0220..0x0224), and repositions items via Item::pop/Item::push, respecting Item::ascend checks and surface-weight constraints.
U8P_PLATFORM::gotHit: if param1 != 0 and Item::isOn(this, param1) == true and Item::getWeightIncludingContents(param1) meets a threshold (inferred >= 10), call PLATFORM::func0080 and suspend.
open_questions: What exact gameplay meaning map to the Item::quality values (0x00/0x01/0x02) — inferred as 'idle/ready/moving' but not explicit.

What is the canonical interpretation of the param/selector passed into U8P_PLATFORM::func0131 (signed direction, number of steps, or mode)? The code appears to convert it to ±2 but details are inferred.
Precise semantics of the type cycle (0x0220..0x0224) — likely visual tile rotation or stage advancement, but exact mapping to in-game assets is unknown.
The exact behavior of PLATFORM::func0080 (hit-response) is not present; how it affects platform state is therefore uncertain.
Edge cases around weight handling: whether heavy weight causes a permanent inversion or a one-time denial is inferred from type/branching but not certain.

main_function.name: U8P_PLATFORM::use
main_function.purpose: Handle player activation of the platform: gate and trigger the platform movement routine when the platform's state and surface weight permit, otherwise provide a denial SFX.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player uses/activates the platform item.
Will early-exit if Item::getQuality(this) == 0x02 (platform locked/special state).
If Item::getQuality(this) == 0x01, the movement routine U8P_PLATFORM::func0131 is invoked immediately.
If quality is other than 0x01/0x02, movement is allowed only when Item::getSurfaceWeight(this) <= 0x64; otherwise a denial sound is played.

function_map.name: U8P_PLATFORM::use
function_map.role: Player activation handler that gates and triggers platform movement

function_map.name: U8P_PLATFORM::gotHit
function_map.role: Respond to external hits on platform; check if the striking object is on the platform and heavy enough, then delegate to a hit-response routine

function_map.name: U8P_PLATFORM::func0131
function_map.role: Core platform movement/rotation routine: set state, play SFX, compute Z offsets, iterate items to push/pop and cycle types, enforce bounds and weight limits

internal_calls.purpose: Trigger the platform's movement/rotation routine when quality/weight checks pass

external_calls.purpose: Read platform state/quality to decide behavior
Check total weight on platform to allow/deny movement (threshold 0x64)
Play denial SFX when movement is disallowed
Test whether the strike source object is on this platform before reacting
Get full object weight (with contents) to compare against hit-trigger threshold
Delegate to a specialized hit-response routine when object-on-platform and weight threshold are met
Mark platform state at start/end of movement (set to 0x02 and later to other values on abort)
Play confirmation SFX on allowed movement and denial SFX on rejected/inverted movement
Read platform X coordinate for repositioning popped items
Read platform Y coordinate for repositioning popped items
Read platform/item Z to compute movement offsets and per-item target heights
Test/attempt upward movement for items; influences iteration direction/feasibility
Obtain the top/anchor item to iterate over items that must be pushed/popped or have types cycled
Read item type to decide how to cycle to the next type during rotation
Change an item's type (cycle among constants) as part of platform visual/behavior rotation
Temporarily push items onto a stack/list while reorganizing them for movement
Reposition popped items at computed (z,y,x) coordinates after movement/type changes

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a movable/interactive platform item. The main 'U8P_PLATFORM::use' process gates platform movement by checking the item's quality/state and the total surface weight on the platform, allowing a movement routine when permitted and playing a denial sound otherwise.
